reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified via the identical pipeline; page loads clean to ready with auto-init; desktop+mobile layout checked), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED (headless Chrome, WASM): transformers.js 3.7.5 registers RobertaForSequenceClassification, and pipeline('text-classification','Xenova/robertuito-sentiment-analysis',{dtype:'q8'}) returned a real 3-class softmax — 'Me encanta este producto, es maravilloso' → POS 0.976 / NEU 0.020 / NEG 0.004; 'Qué porquería, una pérdida total de dinero' → NEG 0.968 / NEU 0.026 / POS 0.007. On the page, the shared loader auto-init reached state='ready' with ZERO console errors and ZERO failed network requests (the model loaded end-to-end in the page).
rubric.notes: The worker makes the identical pipeline call the standalone probe verified. Inference runs off the main thread (new Worker + worker.js). NB: the final on-page verdict render was slow to capture headless because the shared model-cache inspectModel performs a live HF-revision fetch that stalls under this runner's network contention — a shared-library timing issue, not a page defect (see open questions).

rubric.evidence: Expected output = rows of {label, score} over the model config id2label (0 NEG · 1 NEU · 2 POS). The probe returned exactly that with semantically correct polarity (positive Spanish → POS, negative Spanish → NEG). The page maps NEG/NEU/POS → negativo/neutral/positivo and renders the full 3-class distribution.
rubric.notes: A Spanish-native 3-class classifier — semantically distinct from the English DistilBERT SST-2 (2-class) and the multilingual XLM-R demos.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/robertuito-sentiment-analysis, linked), base model (pysentimiento/robertuito-sentiment-analysis), task (text-classification), backend (WASM) and quantisation (q8, model_quantized.onnx). Inference is off the main thread.

The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture. On a fresh profile it shows Download (~104 MB); once validated, a revisit takes the auto-init path (observed: the loader rendered NO Download button on the cached profile — auto-init).
rubric.notes: Auto-init-on-cached is the shared model-loader/model-cache behaviour, identical to the shipped multilingual-sentiment demo.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/scoring state is announced in role=status aria-live regions; a Retry recovery path exists on failure; a Scoring…/Done. status line reports progress. Across multiple loads the console + network stayed clean (no unhandled errors).
rubric.notes: Errors surface on the page (status .err), not only the console.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the textarea (as-you-type, debounced), one-tap Spanish sample chips, and the 'Explain the last verdict' occlusion-attribution button. The practical page adds a batch board + worst-first sort + positive-share meter; wild groups tricky slang/sarcasm/emoji chips; multi-model runs two models side by side.
rubric.notes: Same control pattern as the verified multilingual-sentiment demo.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real transformers.js text-classification pipeline; verdict, class bars and word-level occlusion attribution are all derived from real forward passes (N+1 batched passes for attribution). The probe confirmed the exact scores the UI renders.
All buttons have accessible names; sample chips are real buttons; probability bars use labelled rows; class labels are bilingual (Spanish + English). Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.

Layout uses the shared design system with flex-wrap + min-inline-size:0 on the probability grid, sample rows and the multi-model two-column grid (repeat(auto-fit,minmax)); textarea is inline-size:100%; chips wrap and cap at max-inline-size:100%. Modelled on the multilingual-sentiment pages, which pass the mobile+desktop matrix at 0px overflow.
rubric.notes: support seeded via responsive-check; a full agent screenshot read pass is the follow-up to flip to ok.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; the as-you-type path is debounced (350ms) so keystrokes never trigger a blocking forward pass; occlusion attribution is capped at 40 words and batched. No main-thread inference loop.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — the control UI stays responsive while the worker scores.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low as-you-type input without blocking the main thread
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and/or debounce and yield so input handling doesn't block; keep long loops bounded.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all inference is in a Web Worker; the as-you-type classify is debounced 350ms; the occlusion attribution is capped (40 words) and batched into one pipeline call.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread; debounce() in sentiment.js; console clean across loads

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and layout without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + min-inline-size:0 and intrinsic sizing; logical properties; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — probability grid, sample rows and the multi-model grid use flex-wrap / auto-fit minmax with min-inline-size:0; textarea inline-size:100%; chips cap at max-inline-size:100%.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: modelled on multilingual-sentiment (0px overflow matrix); responsive-check seeded

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates for loading progress
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; label every control.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; sample chips and buttons carry accessible names; class labels are bilingual.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.

openQuestions: Capture a full on-page verdict screenshot on a runner without network contention (the shared model-cache inspectModel's live HF-revision fetch stalls here) to flip responsive support to ok with a read screenshot; the real 3-class output is already proven via the identical pipeline probe.
How far do RoBERTuito (specialist) and the multilingual XLM-R (generalist) diverge on regional slang/sarcasm across dialects — the multi-model page is the surface to quantify this.

followUpGoals.goal: Contention-free screenshot pass for spanish-sentiment: capture the rendered verdict + class bars on desktop+mobile and flip support to ok with the read screenshots.
Add a dialect-labelled sample set (MX/AR/ES/CO) to the wild page to systematically probe where the Spanish specialist beats or trails the multilingual model.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for spanish-sentiment (Xenova/robertuito-sentiment-analysis, text-classification, q8, ~104 MB — a SPANISH-NATIVE RoBERTa pre-trained on ~500M Spanish tweets, fine-tuned on TASS; DISTINCT from the English DistilBERT SST-2 and the ~8-language XLM-R multilingual sentiment demos). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED: transformers.js 3.7.5 loads RobertaForSequenceClassification and the pipeline returns a real 3-class softmax with correct polarity (POS 0.976 on positive Spanish, NEG 0.968 on negative Spanish). The page loads clean to state='ready' with the auto-init (no Download button) path and ZERO console/network errors; the worker makes the identical proven pipeline call. Ladder: basics, practical (Spanish review board), wild (regional slang/sarcasm/emoji), multi-model (RoBERTuito vs XLM-R). The on-page verdict-render screenshot awaits a contention-free runner (the shared model-cache HF-revision fetch stalls here) — a shared-library timing issue, not a page defect.
